sources.name: ONS
sources.dataset: Consumer Price Index — food and non-alcoholic beverages (CPIH)

metadata.methodology: Food inflation figures are the ONS CPI 12-month rate for the food and non-alcoholic beverages category, which covers approximately 200 food and drink items sampled monthly from supermarkets, convenience stores, and specialist food retailers. Category-level price rises compare March 2023 prices to March 2022 prices for specific product groups within the CPI basket. Overall CPI figures are the headline all-items rate.
knownIssues: CPI measures price changes in a fixed basket of goods; if consumers switch to cheaper products in response to price rises ('quality substitution'), CPI overstates the impact on household budgets

Supermarket own-label and value ranges have risen more slowly than branded equivalents, benefiting households that switched; the CPI basket weights these at their market share which may understate branded inflation
Shrinkflation (product size reduction without price change) is not fully captured in official CPI methodology; ONS began publishing a supplementary shrinkflation measure in 2023
